Assembly: The assembly qqTroExca7.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas pilot project~(https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/pilot-project). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. Sample is the homogametic sex but no X chromosome could be identified.

Assembly: The assembly ilDicFasc1.hap1.1 is based on 67x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Assembly: On Oct 10, 2025 this sequence version replaced OU911053.1.; The assembly ilMelMeno1.hap1.4 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The 19 autosomes in Hap1 are named after homology to the previous assembly version. There is a fusion between the W and the previously named autosome 5, the resulting fused chromosome is named W. The non-fused homologous autosome in named Z2. The canonical Z named Z. The 21 autosomes in Hap2 are named based on homology to Hap1. There are 2 heterozygous fission/fusions, the non-fused chromosomes are placed in Hap2 named 1A, 1B, 4A and 4B. Hap1 autosome 2 35.7-37.9 Mb shows high sequence identity to a part of autosome 17 (both haplotypes), likely duplication. This duplication is not present in the homologous Hap2 autosome 2A.

Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of Salmo salar, from a sample provided by Tomasz Podgorniak. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The fSalSal1 collapsed assembly is based on 26x PacBio HiFi, 18x PromethION Oxford Nanopore and 79x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. The Oxford Nanopore data was assembled with different parameters using Flye in multiple runs, and the resulting contigs were merged manually based on alignment to each other. The chromosomes are named according to the established Salmo salar chromosome order.

Assembly: The assembly of Acanthochitona fascicularis (xpAcaFasc5) is based on 37X PacBio data and 171X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 462 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 19.1 Mb (with the largest being 2.6 Mb). Additionally, 904 regions regions totaling 336 Mb (with the largest being 10 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation 61 haplotypic regions and 12 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 28 Mb and 0.92 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 1.5 Mb and 58 kb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly fSalTru1.2 is based on ~68x PacBio data and ~70x coverage 10X Genomics Chromium data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, as well as BioNano data generated at the Rockefeller University Vertebrate Genome Laboratory and ~28x coverage Hi-C data generated by Arima Genomics. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, BioNano hybrid-scaffolding, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named on the following basis: first they were assigned as metacentric versus acrocentric based on a Salmo trutta linkage map and synteny with the Salmo salar assembly GCA_000233375.4; numbers 1 to 11 are metacentric, numbers 12 to 40 are acrocentric, and within each of those two categories they are named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilLasPara1.hap1.1 is based on 43x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly ioSymSang1.hap1.1 is based on 23x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Haplotypic inversions identified on Chromosome 2 from 113-128.1 Mbp, Chromosome 3 from 95-108.5 Mb, Chromosome 6 from 55.7-63 Mb, Chromosome 8 from 9.8-60.7 Mbp and Chromosome 11 from 16.60-38.26Mb. Assembly: This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Vanellus vanellus (bVanVan3.1) is based on 57X of PacBio data and 36X Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 19 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 0.75 Mb (with the largest being 0.28 Mb). Additionally, 353 regions totaling 29 Mb were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed (with the largest being 4 Mb). Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 1 haplotypic regions was removed, totaling 0.74 Mb (with the largest being 0.74 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosome Z was identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

Assembly: The assembly fEcheNa1.2 is based on ~62x PacBio data, ~100x coverage 10X Genomics Chromium data, BioNano data and ~112x coverage Dovetail Hi-C data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, BioNano hybrid-scaffolding, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The mitochondrial assembly was produced at The Rockefeller University using mitoVGP. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny to medaka.

Assembly: The assembly ilColSarp1.hap1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z1, Z2 and W were identified by copy number in the diploid genome as well as HiC signal. Canonical Z was identified by BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).'. The order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome W is uncertain.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Calliactis parasitica. This assembly is provided by the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). Contigs were already mostly chromosome scale. The structure of chromosome 8 is uncertain. Two gaps are located close to the middle, delineating two large regions that could be part of the same chromosome (in which case, the orientation of these two chromosome arms is uncertain) or could represent two different chromosomes. Only one contig of 24Kb was detected as viral and removed.

Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly of Somateria mollissima, common name common eider, based on a sample provided by Morten Helberg. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly bSomMol1.h2.1 is based on 36 PacBio HiFi data, 52x Arima Hi-C data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. No mitochondrial genomes were found in the data. Both W and Z chromosomes are found in hap1. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilYpoPlub1.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny based on Yponomeuta sedellus (moths) GCA_934045075.1. ilYpoPlub1 is a male sample exhibiting the typical ZZ chromosome system. There is a possibility that chromosome 27 in this assembly may be an additional sex chromosome based on its alignment to 27Z in the female assemblies ilYpoCagn1 and ilYpoRore1 both of which have been found to exhibit the sex chrimosome trivalent system of n = 29A + A AW Z as described in~https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/epdf/10.1111/j.1601-5223.1988.tb00311.x . The A component of the trivalent in ilYpoCagn1 and ilYpoRore1 was assigned as chromosome 27Z based on its alignment to chromosome 27 in the closely related species Y.sedellus (ilYpoSedl1 GCA_934045075.1) which is derived from a ZZ male sample. Chromosome 27 in ilYpoPlub1 and Z27 in ilYpoCagn1 and ilYpoRore1 all correspond to Merian element 25.

Assembly: The assembly drHedHeli1.2 is based on 29x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. There is a region of low confidence on Chromosome 1 at 15Mb-26.1Mb consisting of a number of repetitive scaffolds with orientation and order uncertain. HiC data indicates there is a nested heterozygous inversion on Chromosome 3 covering the approximate region 19.7Mb-35.7Mb. On Chromosome 20 there is a heterozygous inversion between approximately 1.6Mb-4.4Mb.

Assembly: The assembly of Atherina boyeri (fAthBoy1) is based on 64X PacBio data and 144X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 1 contig weas identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 28 Kb. Additionally, 234 regions totaling 29.5 Mb (with the largest being 2.7 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 5.1 Mb (with the largest being 1.5 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.
